concepts.title: The ZFC axioms
concepts.blurb: Extensionality, pairing, union, power-set, infinity, separation, replacement, regularity, choice — the nine axioms that pin down which collections are sets and rule out Russell's paradox.

concepts.title: Von Neumann ordinals
concepts.blurb: Each ordinal $\alpha$ is the set of strictly smaller ordinals: $0=\emptyset$, $1=\{0\}$, $2=\{0,1\}$, $\omega=\{0,1,2,\dots\}$. Transfinite induction and recursion run along the well-order $\in$.

concepts.title: Ordinal arithmetic
concepts.blurb: Sum, product, and exponentiation of ordinals defined by transfinite recursion. Non-commutative ($1+\omega=\omega\ne\omega+1$), and the tower $\omega,\omega^\omega,\omega^{\omega^\omega},\dots$ converges to $\varepsilon_0$.

concepts.title: Cardinals and cofinality
concepts.blurb: $|X|$ as the least ordinal in bijection with $X$; the $\aleph$-hierarchy $\aleph_0,\aleph_1,\aleph_2,\dots$; cofinality $\operatorname{cf}(\kappa)$ classifying regular vs singular cardinals; $|\mathbb R|=2^{\aleph_0}$.

concepts.title: The axiom of choice
concepts.blurb: AC, Zorn's lemma, and the well-ordering theorem are equivalent over ZF. Tychonoff's product theorem, existence of a Hamel basis for $\mathbb R/\mathbb Q$, and a non-measurable set all need it.

concepts.title: The continuum hypothesis
concepts.blurb: CH: $2^{\aleph_0}=\aleph_1$. Gödel (1940) showed CH is consistent with ZFC; Cohen (1963) showed $\neg$CH is too — so CH is independent. Forcing is the technique behind Cohen's half.

concepts.title: Inaccessible cardinals
concepts.blurb: An uncountable regular strong-limit cardinal $\kappa$ — closed under power-set and unions of size $<\kappa$. $V_\kappa$ is then a model of ZFC, so existence of one is strictly stronger than ZFC. Grothendieck universes are exactly $V_\kappa$ for $\kappa$ inaccessible.
